correction_notes: Filename field corrected 2026-04-07T19:45 from 'V_8.mov' to 'V18.MOV'. V18.MOV is the 170s desk-mounted RCA monitor recording at SURG25 station (pegboard, 'FLS Circle Cut Training and Testing' sheet). V_8.mov is a distinct 149s wall-mounted RCA recording at SURG20 station and is now scored independently as video_id=V_8_video.
recording_notes: Phone filming desk-mounted RCA monitor at SURG25 station. Pegboard with instruments visible to left. 'SURG 25 - FLS Circle Cut Training and Testing' instruction sheet pinned above monitor. Pink/red foam block with Penrose drain. Dark suture. Oblique monitor angle. 170s total video duration.

frame_analyses.description: ~3.3s: first instrument enters from the left; scoring timer starts here.
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Controlled entry; some extra tail/needle orientation work before a stable load.

frame_analyses.description: ~12.0s: needle being reoriented in front of drain for first bite.
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Deliberate rather than fluid; small regrasp maneuvers cost time.

frame_analyses.description: ~22.0s: needle tip engaged at target, first pass underway.
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Acceptable entry angle; gentle tissue handling.

frame_analyses.description: ~31.0s: needle traversed drain, suture drawn through second side.
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Pass appears close to target; mm accuracy hard to judge from monitor.

frame_analyses.description: ~52.0s: first throw configured with double-wrap appearance.
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Surgeon's knot setup; correct in concept but slowed by regrasping.
Lies down reasonably well, no obvious loosening.

frame_analyses.description: ~84.0s: alternating loop formed near top of field.
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Alternating direction preserved; setup prolonged and uneconomical.

frame_analyses.description: ~108.0s: second throw cinched with tension across knot.
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Stable control; substantial extra motion before seated.

frame_analyses.description: ~146.0s: final required throw formed after tail repositioning.
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Slowest segment; repeated regrasping before advancement.

frame_analyses.description: ~158.0s: knot stack seated, tails oriented for cutting.
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Knot secure; no unraveling or loss of approximation.

frame_analyses.description: ~165.2s: blue cutter dividing remaining tails inside trainer.
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Completed intrabody; transition to scissors was delayed.

knot_assessments.notes: Right-led double throw consistent with surgeon's knot; moderate confidence given monitor angle.
Alternating single throw with opposite instrument leading; prolonged but seats without visible slippage.
Alternates back again and remains secure; prolonged tail management before cut.

drain_assessment.assessment_note: No visible gap seen; closure appears complete.

technique_summary: Task completed successfully with both tails cut inside trainer, no drain avulsion, no knot failure. Needle passage and tissue handling controlled; knot stack held without visible loosening. Main performance limiter was efficiency — needle loading, throw setup, and especially the late knot-tying/tail-management phases were prolonged by repeated regrasping.
improvement_suggestions: Load the needle at a reproducible angle before approaching target.
Keep one tail under constant tension during throws 2 and 3; advance the loop directly to the knot.
Once throw 3 is seated, transition to scissors sooner — post-knot management consumed disproportionate time.
Make the right-left-right hand alternation pattern more obvious and fluid.
strengths: Completed entire sequence including intracorporeal cutting without avulsion.
Controlled instrument-tissue interaction during needle pass and knot advancement.
Knot approximation stable at the end, no unraveling or loss of closure.
comparison_notes: LARGE time delta vs Claude V18 (Δ=17.9s). Claude read 145s (reading from clip-end at frame 23 = 149s), GPT-4o read 162.9s (timing start at ~3s, end at ~166s absolute). ΔFLS = 17.4 — the widest FLS disagreement in V-series so far. Phase-level: Claude consolidates throws into 60s (20/18/22), GPT-4o stretches throws 2+3 across ~97s. Endpoint anchor: Claude's final frame is at 149s absolute and may correspond to GPT-4o's throw-3/cut transition at ~156s absolute, suggesting Claude cut short the end of the clip OR GPT-4o over-counted trailing tail management. Placement and closure agree closely; the disagreement is purely about where 'completion' lands on the timeline.
